sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board changed again. Let me check what moved. The purple square seems to have moved further along the dotted path. Let me track its position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Track purple square position
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'p':
        print(f"purple: id={n['id']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check for white cross
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] < 20:
        print(f"white small: id={n['id']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

The purple square is still at the same position [[52, 9], [52, 11], [54, 11], [54, 9]]. It didn't move. So clicking on the same spot again didn't advance it. 
Let me think about this differently. Maybe I need to click on the next gray dot along the path to move the purple square there. The gray dots form a diagonal path. The purple square is at ~row 53, col 10. The next gray dot along the path toward the blue circle would be at a higher row and higher column position.
Wait, looking at the gray dots from before:
- [55, 8], [57, 6] - these are BELOW the purple square
- [51, 12], [49, 14], [47, 16], ... going up toward the blue circle
The path goes from bottom-left to top-right. The purple square is at row 53, col 10. The next dot toward the blue circle would be at [51, 12].
Let me try clicking on the gray dot at [51, 12] to move the purple square there.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The purple square is stuck at [52-54, 9-11]. Clicking same spot didn't move it.
# The gray dots form a diagonal path. Next dot toward blue circle is at [51, 12].
# Let me try clicking on the next gray dot along the path.
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 51, 'col': 12}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
